output.summary: Three changes to Wide Social Media block: (1) Fix right-alignment of links when heading is empty by adding md:ml-auto to the <nav> element, (2) Add showLabel boolean attribute (default true) on the parent block, passed via block context to child items, controlling label visibility, (3) When showLabel is false, add a title attribute to the <a> element in the child item so the social media name is available on hover.

fileChanges.description: Add showLabel boolean attribute (default true) and update providesContext to pass it to child blocks.
Fix right-alignment of nav when heading is empty, add ToggleControl for showLabel in InspectorControls.
fileChanges.details: 1) Add md:ml-auto class to the <nav> element so links are always right-aligned regardless of heading presence. 2) Add InspectorControls import and a ToggleControl for the showLabel attribute. 3) Update the WideSocialMediaAttributes type to include showLabel?: boolean.

fileChanges.description: Fix right-alignment of nav when heading is empty.
fileChanges.details: Add 'md:ml-auto' class to the <nav> element. This ensures that when no heading is rendered, the nav is pushed to the right side of the flex container. The parent div already has md:justify-between which works when both heading and nav are present, but when heading is absent the nav needs ml-auto to stay right-aligned.

fileChanges.description: Add usesContext to receive showLabel from parent block.
fileChanges.details: Add "usesContext": ["takt/wide-social-media/showLabel"] to the child block's block.json.

fileChanges.description: Conditionally show/hide label based on context, add title attribute to link when label is hidden.
fileChanges.details: 1) Add context to EditProps type: context: { 'takt/wide-social-media/showLabel'?: boolean }. 2) Read showLabel from context (default true). 3) Conditionally render the label RichText only when showLabel is true. 4) When showLabel is false, add a title attribute to the <a> element using the label value (attributes.label) so the name appears on hover. 5) In the editor, always show the label RichText for editing purposes but add a visual indicator (e.g., reduced opacity) when showLabel is false.

fileChanges.description: Conditionally render label text and add title attribute to link when label is hidden.
fileChanges.details: 1) Read showLabel from block context: $showLabel = $block->context['takt/wide-social-media/showLabel'] ?? true. 2) Wrap the label <span> in a conditional: only render when $showLabel is true. 3) When $showLabel is false and $label is not empty, add title="{$label}" to the <a> element. 4) When $showLabel is false and label is empty but linkTitle is available, use linkTitle as the title attribute instead.

attributeChanges.name: showLabel
attributeChanges.description: Controls whether the social media name/label text is visible next to the icon. When false, a title attribute is added to the link for hover visibility.

contextChanges.description: Pass showLabel boolean from parent to child items so all items respect the same visibility setting.

designDecisions.decision: Add md:ml-auto to the <nav> element to ensure right-alignment when heading is absent
designDecisions.evidence: User reported links are not right-aligned when heading is empty. The flex container uses md:justify-between which requires two children to push the second right. With only the nav present, ml-auto achieves the desired right-alignment.
designDecisions.recommendation: md:ml-auto on the <nav> is the cleanest CSS-only fix. It works both with and without the heading present.

designDecisions.decision: Place showLabel attribute on the parent block (not child) and pass via context, so all items share the same visibility setting
designDecisions.evidence: User wants a single toggle to show/hide all labels. Placing on parent avoids per-item toggles and ensures consistency. This follows the same context pattern used by CardsCarousel (columns), CardsGrid (columns), and MasonryCards (masonryStyle).
designDecisions.recommendation: Parent-level attribute with context is the established pattern in this codebase.

designDecisions.decision: When showLabel is false, use the item's label attribute value as the link title. Fall back to link.title if label is empty.
designDecisions.evidence: User request #3 specifies adding title attribute so users can see the name on hover. The label attribute is the most user-facing name (e.g., 'Facebook'), while link.title may contain a longer description.
designDecisions.recommendation: Priority: label > link.title for the title attribute value.

designDecisions.decision: In the editor (TSX), always show the label RichText for editing even when showLabel is false, but with reduced opacity to indicate it will be hidden on the frontend
designDecisions.evidence: Editors need to be able to set/edit the label text even when it is hidden on the frontend. Showing it with visual distinction (opacity) communicates the hidden state while keeping it editable.
designDecisions.recommendation: Use opacity-50 on the label span in editor when showLabel is false.

rightAlignmentFix.problem: Parent div uses `md:flex-row md:justify-between`. When heading is not rendered, the <nav> is the only flex child and `justify-between` has no effect, causing left-alignment.
rightAlignmentFix.solution: Add `md:ml-auto` to the <nav> element. With heading present, justify-between still works normally. Without heading, ml-auto pushes nav to the right.

showLabelToggle.help: When disabled, only icons are shown. Names appear on hover.

titleAttribute.condition: showLabel is false AND (label OR linkTitle is not empty)
titleAttribute.phpImplementation: $titleAttr = !$showLabel ? ($label ?: $linkTitle) : ''; Add title="{$titleAttr}" to <a> when not empty.
titleAttribute.tsxImplementation: Add title prop to <a> when showLabel is false: title={!showLabel ? (attributes.label || attributes.link?.title || '') : undefined}

newVariations.demoDescription: showLabel is false. Icons display without text labels. Hover over each icon to see the title tooltip with the social media name.
newVariations.purpose: Tests the new showLabel=false behavior with title attributes on links

newVariations.demoDescription: showLabel is false and no heading. Tests right-alignment of icon-only links without heading.
newVariations.purpose: Tests both fixes together: right-alignment without heading + hidden labels

newVariations.demoDescription: Default behavior with showLabel=true. Labels should display normally.
newVariations.purpose: Regression test ensuring default behavior is unchanged

testPageUpdates.existingVariationPurpose: Verify the right-alignment fix: links should now be right-aligned even without a heading

qaChecklist: Links are right-aligned on desktop when heading is empty
Links are right-aligned on desktop when heading is present (no regression)
Mobile layout stacks correctly regardless of heading presence
showLabel=true shows label text (default behavior unchanged)
showLabel=false hides label text on frontend
showLabel=false adds title attribute to <a> element with label value
Hovering over icon-only link shows tooltip with social media name
Accessibility: aria-label still present on links when showLabel is false
Editor shows label with reduced opacity when showLabel is false
